steps.title: Use the circle radii
steps.content: Since $P$ is the center and $Q$ and $R$ lie on the circle, $PQ$ and $PR$ are both radii. Each has length $10$, so the perimeter equation has only $QR$ unknown.

steps.title: Identify the radii
steps.content: Since $Q$ and $R$ lie on the circle with center $P$ and radius 10 inches, both $PQ$ and $PR$ are radii of the circle. Therefore, $PQ = 10$ and $PR = 10$.

steps.title: Apply the perimeter constraint
steps.content:
The perimeter of triangle $PQR$ is the sum of all three sides:
$$PQ + PR + QR = 34$$
Substituting the known values:
$$10 + 10 + QR = 34$$
$$20 + QR = 34$$
$$QR = 14$$
That confirms B is correct.
